title: Wildcards in Parameters
text: Wildcards in Parameters A number of the command line switches recognize a limited set of wildcards in parameters. Table 1. Wildcards for Parameters of Command Line Switches Wildcard Meaning * or % Match 0- n characters ? or _ Match one character ! Logical NOT Some operating systems attach their own interpretation to these characters, so using them can require special care. In some cases, your operating system provides a way of ensuring that wildcard characters are passed to Uniface without being interpreted by the operating system. Often, enclosing the parameters containing wildcards in double quotation marks (") is effective. Operating System Rules Observe the conventions of your operating system. Some of the information provided with parameters can be misunderstood by your operating system. For example, Unix does not recognize the logon information provided with the /log switch unless you enclose the logon information in double quotation marks ("). Related Links Uniface Command Line Interface Uniface provides a command line interface that enables you to access many functions without actually opening the user interface. By running a Uniface executable (such as ide.exe or uniface) with command line switches you can compile objects, debug applications, import and export, specify assignment files, and more.
